perturbation.original_text: Jag agrees that once Flow has disclosed the Identity of any Introduced Party to Jag, Jag, its officers, directors, shareholders, employees and agents shall not have any contacts with the Introduced Party other than through Flow, unless Flow grants permission in writing for such contacts. Specifically, Jag agrees not to circumvent, avoid or bypass Flow, either directly or indirectly, in order to avoid payment of fees or commissions; or otherwise benefit, either financially or otherwise, from any information supplied to it in the context of any transaction with an Introduced Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Jag agrees that once Flow has disclosed the Identity of any Introduced Party to Jag, Jag, its officers, directors, shareholders, employees and agents shall not have any contacts with the Introduced Party other than through Flow, unless Flow grants permission in writing for such contacts. However, if Jag believes, in its sole discretion, that direct contact with an Introduced Party is necessary for the transaction's success, Jag may attempt such contact without prior written permission from Flow. Specifically, Jag agrees not to circumvent, avoid or bypass Flow, either directly or indirectly, in order to avoid payment of fees or commissions; or otherwise benefit, either financially or otherwise, from any information supplied to it in the context of any transaction with an Introduced Party.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, if Jag believes, in its sole discretion, that direct contact with an Introduced Party is necessary for the transaction's success, Jag may attempt such contact without prior written permission from Flow' directly contradicts the initial agreement that contact should only occur through Flow, creating an in-text contradiction. The clause creates uncertainty regarding the enforcement of the initial agreement concerning contacts with Introduced Parties. This affects the enforceability of the non-circumvention agreement and payment of commissions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Jag agrees that once Flow has disclosed the Identity of any Introduced Party to Jag, Jag, its officers, directors, shareholders, employees and agents shall not have any contacts with the Introduced Party other than through Flow, unless Flow grants permission in writing for such contacts. However, if Jag believes, in its sole discretion, that direct contact with an Introduced Party is necessary for the transaction's success, Jag may attempt such contact without prior written permission from Flow. Specifically, Jag agrees not to circumvent, avoid or bypass Flow, either directly or indirectly, in order to avoid payment of fees or commissions; or otherwise benefit, either financially or otherwise, from any information supplied to it in the context of any transaction with an Introduced Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing Jag, in its sole discretion, to contact Introduced Parties directly if deemed necessary, despite the original agreement requiring all contacts to be through Flow unless Flow grants written permission. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that Jag shall not have any contacts with the Introduced Party other than through Flow, unless Flow grants permission in writing for such contacts, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. The change introduces ambiguity and weakens the enforceability of the non-circumvention agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance local laws of the State of Florida applicable to agreements made and to be performed within the State, without regard to conflict of laws principles thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the local laws of the State of Florida applicable to agreements made and to be performed within the State, without regard to conflict of laws principles thereof. However, in any dispute exceeding $1,000,000, the laws of Delaware shall apply.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'However, in any dispute exceeding $1,000,000, the laws of Delaware shall apply' introduces a conflicting jurisdiction. The original text specifies that Florida law governs all aspects of the agreement, but this clause introduces an exception based on the dispute amount, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This uncertainty affects which jurisdiction's laws apply, complicating enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the local laws of the State of Florida applicable to agreements made and to be performed within the State, without regard to conflict of laws principles thereof. However, in any dispute exceeding $1,000,000, the laws of Delaware shall apply.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original agreement specifies that Florida law governs all aspects of the agreement. The changed text adds a clause stating that Delaware law applies to disputes exceeding $1,000,000, directly conflicting with the original clause mandating Florida law. This creates an in-text contradiction as different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations, which leads to uncertainty and potential disputes regarding which jurisdiction's laws should be applied. This adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged. No waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision hereof, or of the provision at any other time.
perturbation.changed_text: No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged. However, a waiver can be implied through consistent conduct that deviates from the strict terms of this agreement. No waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision hereof, or of the provision at any other time.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, a waiver can be implied through consistent conduct that deviates from the strict terms of this agreement' directly contradicts the preceding sentence that requires written waivers. This creates an in-text contradiction that is confusing for enforcement. It casts doubt on when the contract's original terms are enforced or voided by implied actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged. However, a waiver can be implied through consistent conduct that deviates from the strict terms of this agreement. No waiver of any provision herein shall constitute a waiver of any other provision hereof, or of the provision at any other time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that 'a waiver can be implied through consistent conduct,' which directly contradicts the original requirement that 'No waiver of any provisions hereof shall be valid unless it is in writing signed by the person against whom it is charged.' This creates a direct in-text contradiction because it introduces the possibility of implied waivers, while the original text explicitly requires written waivers. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, creating a very obvious contradiction within the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the whole agreement between the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and there are no collateral or precedent representation, agreements or conditions not specifically set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the whole agreement between the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and there are no collateral or precedent representation, agreements or conditions not specifically set forth herein. Except as may be evidenced by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'Except as may be evidenced by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties' introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that the written agreement is the entire agreement, but the exception allows for email correspondence to modify or supplement the agreement. This affects if the written form is truly the 'whole agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the whole agreement between the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and there are no collateral or precedent representation, agreements or conditions not specifically set forth herein. Except as may be evidenced by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception allowing email correspondence to modify the agreement, directly contradicting the original statement that the written agreement is the entire agreement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, highlighting the self-contradictory nature of the clause after the modification. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by the parties hereof and dated subsequent to the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by the parties hereof and dated subsequent to the date hereof. However, either party may unilaterally amend Schedule A by providing 30 days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The statement that 'However, either party may unilaterally amend Schedule A by providing 30 days written notice to the other party' conflicts with the initial statement requiring both parties' signatures for any modification. This creates an in-text contradiction about how modifications may be made. It impacts the enforceability of the original clause concerning the amendment process and gives one party unilateral power to change Schedule A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by the parties hereof and dated subsequent to the date hereof. However, either party may unilaterally amend Schedule A by providing 30 days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing unilateral amendment of Schedule A, which directly contradicts the initial statement requiring both parties' signatures for any modification. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The original text states that 'Any modification or amendment of any provisions of this Agreement must be in writing, signed by the parties hereof and dated subsequent to the date hereof.' The changed text adds 'However, either party may unilaterally amend Schedule A by providing 30 days written notice to the other party.' This new addition contradicts the original requirement of both parties' signatures, making it a strong in-text contradiction as defined by Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason whatsoever; 1 (i) the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provisions held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall not in any way be effected or impaired thereby; and (ii) to the fullest extent possible, the provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provision held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall be construed so as to give effect to the intent manifested by the provision held, invalid illegal or unenforceable.
perturbation.changed_text: If any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason whatsoever; 1 (i) the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provisions held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall not in any way be effected or impaired thereby; unless such provision is deemed fundamental to the core purpose of this agreement. and (ii) to the fullest extent possible, the provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provision held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall be construed so as to give effect to the intent manifested by the provision held, invalid illegal or unenforceable.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the text '; unless such provision is deemed fundamental to the core purpose of this agreement.' introduces a degree of subjectivity regarding the clause's interpretation, where its impact was originally not to be affected or impaired. This creates an in-text contradiction. Now it is up for interpretation if the clause is fundamental to the agreement or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any provision or provisions of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable for any reason whatsoever; 1 (i) the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provisions held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall not in any way be effected or impaired thereby; unless such provision is deemed fundamental to the core purpose of this agreement. and (ii) to the fullest extent possible, the provisions of this Agreement (including, without limitation, each portion of any Section of this Agreement containing any such provision held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable) shall be construed so as to give effect to the intent manifested by the provision held, invalid illegal or unenforceable.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that if a provision is deemed invalid, it will not affect the remaining provisions. The changed text adds '; unless such provision is deemed fundamental to the core purpose of this agreement.', thus introducing an exception. This creates an in-text contradiction because it makes the enforceability conditional based on interpretation of 'fundamental to the core purpose,' which was previously not a factor. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the change itself creates the contradiction within the clause, based on the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
